metric_name: Viasoft c.era long-term viability beyond Y2K

metric_value: After Y2K deadline passed revenues collapsed; Viasoft acquired by Allen Systems Group for $152M in April 2000 ($8.40/share) — stock had traded at much higher levels in 1997-1998
notes: Y2K market evaporated post-Jan 2000; Viasoft's post-Y2K pivot to application asset management failed. Source: https://www.nytimes.com/2000/04/29/business/company-news-allen-systems-a-software-developer-to-get-viasoft.html

metric_value: Project scope typically greater than anticipated; impact on each department and system is very different

metric_value: Y2K remediation was an ~$600 billion global effort (est.); heterogeneous multi-vendor approaches were indeed required; no catastrophic failures occurred
notes: Aberdeen's scope warning was directionally correct; Y2K was massive. Source: https://www.encyclopedia.com/books/politics-and-business-magazines/viasoft-inc
